testName: should login with valid credentials to LMS, and load a skill, play, pause, and resume the first video

fix.summary: Multiple sidebar close buttons can render in the SCORM-nested learn view. Close every visible matching control, wait until none are displayed, then trigger playback via document.querySelector + JS click instead of WebDriver click on the custom element.
fix.pattern: Replace single combined $() close with: const closeBtns = await $$('[aria-label="Close content sidebar"], [aria-label="Close sidebar"]'); for (const btn of closeBtns) { if (await btn.isDisplayed()) { await btn.waitForClickable({ timeout: 10000 }); await btn.click(); } } Then: await browser.waitUntil(async () => !(await $$('[aria-label="Close content sidebar"], [aria-label="Close sidebar"]')).some(async b => await b.isDisplayed()), { timeout: 10000, timeoutMsg: 'Sidebar still visible' }). Replace mux-player waitForClickable + element click with: await browser.execute(() => document.querySelector('mux-player')?.click()). For SCORM iframe contexts, extend mux-player waitForExist timeout to 60000.

investigationFindings: Triage PR #281 / commit 0e2078a: failure surfaced as clickability timeout; root cause is sidebar/chrome covering the player in SCORM-nested learn view. The SCORM-iframe context makes mux mount slower, so a 60s waitForExist is appropriate here even though most flows run with the 15s default.
rootCauseChain: ELEMENT_VISIBILITY -> sidebar overlay covers mux-player -> WebDriver click intercepted -> 10s timeout
